name: Delta-V
description: 2-player tactical space combat game on a hex grid. Ships move with realistic inertia; win by landing on the opponent's objective body or destroying their fleet.

scenarios.name: Bi-Planetary
scenarios.description: 1v1 corvettes race to land on the opponent's world

scenarios.name: Escape
scenarios.description: Pilgrim transport and escorts flee Enforcer interceptors

scenarios.name: Lunar Evacuation
scenarios.description: Evacuate Luna before the enemy fleet overruns it

scenarios.name: Convoy
scenarios.description: Escort or interdict a convoy to Venus

scenarios.name: Duel
scenarios.description: Frigates clash near Mercury — last ship standing wins

scenarios.name: Blockade Runner
scenarios.description: Packet ship races past a corvette to reach Mars

scenarios.name: Interplanetary War
scenarios.description: Build your fleet with MegaCredits — total war across the solar system

scenarios.name: Fleet Action
scenarios.description: Build your fleet and clash — Mars vs Venus

scenarios.name: Grand Tour
scenarios.description: Race past every major body in the solar system and return home

endpoints.description: Create a private game room. Returns a 5-character code and a playerToken for the host.
Enqueue for public matchmaking. Returns a ticket to poll. Prefix playerKey with 'agent_' to tag as a bot.

player.playerKey: string — stable 8-64 char ID [A-Za-z0-9_-], prefix with agent_ for bots

endpoints.description: Poll ticket status. Poll every 500ms-2s. Returns queued, matched, or expired.
Check whether a room exists and has an open seat. Returns room metadata.
Fetch full replay timeline for a completed game. Optional query params: playerToken, gameId.
WebSocket game connection. Add ?playerToken=... to join as a player; omit to spectate.

websocketProtocol.format: JSON, all messages have a 'type' discriminant field

serverToClient.when: Submitted action failed its ActionGuards check
serverToClient.keyFields: reason, message, expected, actual, state, idempotencyKey?

actionGuards.description: Optional fields attachable to any C2S action so the server can reject stale/duplicate submissions with a fresh state instead of the action silently landing on the wrong turn.

fields.expectedTurn: integer — reject if the authoritative turn has advanced
fields.expectedPhase: Phase — reject if the authoritative phase has advanced
fields.idempotencyKey: string (1-128 chars) — server rejects with `duplicateIdempotencyKey` if the same key was seen this phase

actionGuards.rejectionMessage: S2C actionRejected { reason, message, expected, actual, state }

mcp.description: The preferred integration path for autonomous agents. Local stdio MCP is shipping; remote HTTP MCP is on the roadmap (see AGENT_SPEC.md).

actionResult.description: delta_v_send_action({ waitForResult: true }) blocks until the next state-bearing S2C or actionRejected and returns an ActionResult: { accepted, turnApplied, phaseApplied, nextTurn, nextPhase, effects[], nextObservation? }. Pass includeNextObservation: true to embed a fresh observation (same v2 toggles as delta_v_get_observation).

agentInterface.description: The LLM player bridge (scripts/llm-player.ts) wraps the WebSocket and exposes a simpler stdin/stdout or HTTP interface to your agent. The shape matches delta_v_get_observation so the same agent code works via either path.

inputSchema.tactical: optional (v2) — derived features: nearestEnemyDistance, fuelAdvantage, objectiveDistance, enemyObjectiveDistance, threatAxis, turnsToObjective
inputSchema.spatialGrid: optional (v2) — ASCII hex grid with legend, fog-of-war compliant
inputSchema.labeledCandidates: optional (v2) — enriched candidate list with label, reasoning, risk (low/medium/high)

outputSchema.candidateIndex: number — index into candidates[] (REQUIRED, or omit for recommended)

agentInterface.fallback: On timeout or invalid response the bridge uses recommendedIndex

autonomousLoop.description: Recommended high-level turn loop for autonomous agents.
steps: Queue with POST /quick-match and a stable player.playerKey prefixed by agent_
On each state message, act only when phase + activePlayer permit
Send one legal C2S action for the exact current phase

quickStart.noCloneRequired: POST /quick-match with a player object, poll until matched, connect WebSocket, respond to phase messages
quickStart.withBridge: git clone https://github.com/tre-systems/delta-v && npm install && npm run llm:player -- --mode create --scenario duel --agent command --agent-command 'python ./my_agent.py'
quickStart.claudeAgent: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... npm run llm:player -- --mode create --agent command --agent-command 'npm run llm:agent:claude'
quickStart.groqAgent: GROQ_API_KEY=... npm run llm:player -- --mode create --agent command --agent-command 'npm run llm:agent:groq'

botConvention.description: Prefix your playerKey with 'agent_' (e.g. 'agent_my-bot-v1-abc123') to identify your connection as a bot in server logs and replays.
